pattern: Direct topic search on GitHub yields high relevance for framework discovery
heuristic: Use gh search repos with exact topic phrases like 'self-evolving agent'. Single query can yield 20+ repos.

pattern: Exact phrase search on GitHub for narrow concepts
heuristic: Quoted phrases like '"self-evolving AI agent"' produce highly relevant repos. Works better than broad terms.

pattern: DDGS with year qualifier boosts freshness
heuristic: Add year (e.g. '2025') to DDGS queries when stale content dominates. Requires Python 3.11+ for SSL.

pattern: arXiv all: search returns many unrelated papers
heuristic: Use title-only search for precision. all: field matches abstract and full text, producing tangential results (ASR, colonoscopy, dark energy).

pattern: sort=relevance gives 5x better pain ratio than sort=top
heuristic: Always use sort=relevance for pain point discovery. sort=top returns popular content, not problem-relevant content. Also: always use restrict_sr=on to avoid r/all results.

pattern: HN requires quoted product names
heuristic: '"Claude Code"' in quotes = 16,600 score. Without quotes or with modifiers = <50. 100x difference. HN users use product names, not abstract concepts.

pattern: Exa beats gh search for GitHub issue discovery
heuristic: Exa found 8 perfect GitHub issues in one query. gh search found 0 for same topic. Always use Exa for issue discovery.

pattern: Use symptom language, not emotional language
heuristic: 'ignores CLAUDE.md' finds pain points. 'frustrating CLAUDE.md' finds nothing useful. Specific symptoms > emotional adjectives. Also: avoid first-person narrative ('I told it') — matches bragging, not complaints.

pattern: NEW_urls × avg_engagement prevents gaming
heuristic: volume × avg_engagement is gamed by hot posts appearing for every query. Use NEW unique URLs × avg_engagement instead.

pattern: Filter Show HN by points >= 100
heuristic: Show HN posts with <100 points are usually self-promotion, not community-validated content.

pattern: HuggingFace API max 2 keywords
heuristic: HF API with 3+ keywords returns empty. Keep to 1-2 core terms. When you find a good dataset, check the author's other datasets and HF Collections.

pattern: Early stopping after 5 rounds with <10% improvement
heuristic: 5 consecutive rounds with <10% improvement reliably signals convergence. 3 was too aggressive — caused premature stops.

pattern: Twitter native search unreliable
heuristic: xreach search returns empty for all query types. Use Exa with site:twitter.com instead.

pattern: Own-knowledge adds depth search APIs miss
heuristic: Foundational works (STaR, Reflexion, Voyager, LATS, CRITIC) from Claude's training data add depth that no API search surfaces. Always use use-own-knowledge.md for established fields.

pattern: WebSearch results lack date metadata
heuristic: Must explicitly extract dates from arXiv IDs (2403.xxxxx = 2024-03), GitHub updatedAt, URL path date segments, snippet text patterns. Without this, freshness dimension scores near zero.

pattern: 120s latency budget too tight for multi-platform
heuristic: Multi-platform search with sequential tool calls needs 300s+. Either increase budget or parallelize searches.

pattern: Date extraction in LLM evaluation improves freshness 867%
heuristic: Adding date extraction guidance to llm-evaluate.md improved freshness 0.076 → 0.660. The data exists in results but evaluator must be explicitly told to extract it.

pattern: Query diversity is load-bearing
heuristic: Removing dimension variety from gene-query.md caused quantity, diversity, AND relevance to all drop simultaneously. Never restrict to single dimension.

pattern: AVO commit/revert mechanism validated
heuristic: Git commit on score improvement + git revert on regression works correctly. Skill-level modification is the right granularity for AVO evolution (not config weights).

pattern: 9th dimension (Recent Developments) in systematic-recall improves freshness and diversity
heuristic: Adding an explicit recency-focused dimension to systematic-recall forces the agent to recall recent work first, which increases both freshness (+0.063) and diversity (+0.172) because recent items come from more varied sources. Also makes gap-driven queries target freshness gaps more precisely.

pattern: v2.4 skills (systematic-recall, knowledge-map) are evolvable by AVO
heuristic: AVO can successfully modify v2.4 mutable skills and produce measurable score improvements. The systematic-recall dimension set is the right abstraction level for evolution -- adding/modifying dimensions changes recall behavior which cascades to search and scoring.

pattern: Cumulative evidence hurts efficiency score
heuristic: When bundling evidence across sessions cumulatively, query count grows but each new session adds fewer unique URLs. Efficiency = unique_urls / (queries * 3) drops. Either reset query count per session or weight recent queries higher.

pattern: Knowledge map enables targeted gap-only queries
heuristic: With prior knowledge map, Session 2 generated queries ONLY for LOW/GAP items instead of rediscovering known entities. Session 3 even more targeted. Queries per new URL improved: S1=17q/30url, S2=18q/20url, S3=12q/12url.

pattern: Freshness improves with targeted recent-content queries
heuristic: Explicitly searching for '2026 March' and 'latest developments' in Session 3 improved freshness from 0.167 to 0.290. Adding year qualifiers to every gap-filling query is high-value.

pattern: Latency dimension needs session-scoped timing
heuristic: Using shared timing.json across sessions breaks latency scoring for comparison experiments. Each evidence file needs its own timing or the judge should allow per-file timing override.

pattern: Knowledge map dimension coverage reveals blind spots early
heuristic: Session 1 map had only 3 key-people entries and 2 risks entries. This structural gap was visible immediately and drove Session 2 queries. Without the map, these gaps would not have been detected.

pattern: Own-knowledge entries provide depth but hurt freshness
heuristic: Own-knowledge items (foundational papers, established frameworks) are HIGH relevance but often have dates before the 183-day freshness window. Must balance with recent search results.

pattern: paper-list-channel-for-citation-queries
heuristic: When a query asks for papers that cite or extend a foundational work, search GitHub for curated paper-list repos (awesome-lists, survey paper collections) and extract papers from their READMEs. This adds a paper-list source class that improves diversity score. Filter extracted papers to recent 2 years to avoid freshness regression.

pattern: Chinese AI coding tool evaluations are uniquely available on Zhihu
heuristic: Search zhihu.com for AI编程工具评测 to find domestic tool comparisons not available in English sources. Discovers tools like Trae, Tongyi Lingma, InsCode.

pattern: SWE-bench variant papers cluster on arxiv with predictable IDs
heuristic: Search for 'SWE-bench' directly on arxiv to find the growing family of benchmark papers (++, Live, Pro, EVO, Next). High discovery rate for benchmark evolution.

pattern: Gap-driven queries for market data yield concrete numbers
heuristic: Queries like 'AI coding tool market share revenue 2026' on general web yield specific ARR/funding figures that fill commercial knowledge gaps effectively.

pattern: METR study and productivity paradox highly discoverable
heuristic: Searching 'AI coding productivity study' on web surfaces the METR RCT and related research easily. High-impact discovery for any AI coding topic.

pattern: ProductHunt categories page gives landscape overview
heuristic: ProductHunt's /categories/ai-coding-agents gives a ranked view of tools by community rating. Good for discovering new entrants.

pattern: github-repos sorted by stars is far more valuable than default search — discovered 13 projects >44 stars vs default which found mostly 0-star repos
semantic-scholar rate limits at 429 on second query — need retry logic or delay between calls
reddit channel not configured in CHANNEL_METHODS — needs to be added as ddgs site:reddit.com
zhihu via ddgs site:zhihu.com returns off-topic results for English technical queries — Chinese queries also poor, likely blocked by ddgs
producthunt via ddgs site:producthunt.com returned 0 results — needs dedicated API or different search approach
gh search repos with --sort stars is the most effective way to find significant projects — better than search_runner's github-repos channel
foundational papers from training knowledge need arxiv URLs added — check-rubrics will fail r023 without them
Rich HTML with embedded Mermaid diagrams, mechanism cards, timeline, and details/summary achieves 100% rubric pass rate on first check — invest in delivery quality, not just search quantity

Reusing prior session evidence (80 records) then running 8 targeted gap-filling queries is 3x more efficient than restarting search from scratch — always check worklog for prior sessions on same topic
zhihu channel via search_runner returns 5-6 relevant Chinese results per session for AI topics — worth including for Chinese deliveries even if hit rate is 30-40%
Two-stage citation lock (compile reference list before writing, then use only [N] numbers during synthesis) eliminates URL fabrication and passes presentation rubrics automatically
